attack_chain.description: Establish a trusted relationship with a contractor or business partner to gain access to the network.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Steal or forge authentication certificates from the contractor or business partner to gain access to the network.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Use the stolen or forged authentication certificates to obtain valid accounts on the network.
attack_chain.mitigation: Multi-factor Authentication: Enforce MFA for all remote access and privileged accounts. Monitor for anomalous login patterns, impossible travel, and credential stuffing.

attack_chain.description: Compromise the valid accounts obtained in the previous step to gain elevated privileges.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Use alternate authentication material, such as stolen or forged credentials, to access sensitive areas of the network.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Use remote services, such as RDP or SSH, to move laterally within the network.
attack_chain.mitigation: Multi-factor Authentication: Enforce MFA for remote services (SMB, RDP, WinRM). Restrict lateral movement paths using network segmentation and privileged access workstations.

attack_chain.description: Use the compromised accounts and elevated privileges to steal financial information from the network.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Use alternate authentication material to maintain access to the network and avoid detection.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.

attack_chain.description: Continue to use remote services to move laterally within the network and maintain access to sensitive areas.
attack_chain.mitigation: Multi-factor Authentication: Enforce MFA for remote services (SMB, RDP, WinRM). Restrict lateral movement paths using network segmentation and privileged access workstations.

attack_chain.description: Continue to steal financial information from the network using the compromised accounts and elevated privileges.
attack_chain.mitigation: General Best Practices: Apply defense-in-depth principles: network segmentation, least-privilege access, endpoint detection and response (EDR), security awareness training, regular patching, and monitoring.
